title: The farmer's son became a Millionaire in Africa using 'The Shark Mentality'
Timbuktu: The African University That Shocked the World || Untold Africa
description:
For centuries, the world was told that Africa had no history, no scholarship, no intellectual tradition.

But in the heart of the Sahara, a city called Timbuktu was home to one of the greatest centers of learning in the world.

At its height between the 14th and 16th centuries, Timbuktu housed thousands of scholars and an estimated hundreds of thousands of manuscripts covering astronomy, mathematics, medicine, law, and theology.

This is the story of Sankoré University, the manuscript families who preserved knowledge through invasion, and the intellectual empire that history almost forgot.

Untold Africa is a Masterminds original documentary series exploring the powerful stories history left behind.

Welcome to Untold Africa.

References

• Hunwick, John O. Timbuktu and the Songhay Empire (Brill, 1999)
• Jeppie, Shamil & Diagne, Souleymane Bachir (eds.). The Meanings of Timbuktu (HSRC Press, 2008)
• National Geographic, “Saving Timbuktu’s Ancient Manuscripts”
• The Ahmed Baba Institute of Higher Islamic Studies and Research (Timbuktu)
• UNESCO World Heritage Centre – Timbuktu
• Hall, Bruce S. A History of Race in Muslim West Africa, 1600–1960
• Saad, Elias N. Social History of Timbuktu (Cambridge University Press)

title: How to Make Easy Money in Ghana | Ama Burland

description:

One flag. One army. One destiny.

In this episode of Untold Africa, we explore the rise, triumph, vision, and controversial fall of the man who dared to imagine a united continent, and nearly made it happen.

From Nkroful to America.
From prison to presidency.
From independence to exile.

Kwame Nkrumah reshaped African history, and shook the foundations of empire.

Untold Africa is a Masterminds original series uncovering the powerful stories history left behind.

They were called Kandakes.

And they did not bow.

In this episode of Untold Africa, a Masterminds documentary series, we explore the true story of the Warrior Queens of Nubia, African rulers who led armies, built pyramids, advanced iron industries, and forced the Roman Empire into negotiation.

When Rome conquered Egypt in 30 BCE, it looked south toward Kush.

Queen Amanirenas responded with war.

Kushite forces stormed Roman outposts.
They seized territory.
They carried off the bronze head of Emperor Augustus as a symbol of defiance.

For years, Rome fought to subdue Nubia.

It failed.

The final treaty demanded no tribute.
Rome withdrew.
Kush remained free.

But their power was not only military.

The Kandakes ruled as sovereign leaders.
They commissioned temples.
They built the pyramids of Meroë.
They advanced ironworking, architecture, and trade.

Long before the world debated gender equality,
African queens were commanding nations.

This episode of Untold Africa challenges the idea that power in the ancient world belonged only to Rome or Egypt.

The Warrior Queens of Nubia were not myths.

They were strategy.
They were fire.
They were sovereignty.

This is Masterminds — where we uncover the leaders and empires that shaped Africa’s rise.

And remember…

You come from greatness.

In 1324, his pilgrimage to Mecca shocked the global economy. His generosity in Cairo caused gold prices to collapse for years. European cartographers redrew their maps. Africa was no longer a blank space, it was a center of power.

But Mansa Musa’s legacy was not just wealth.

He transformed gold into knowledge.
He built mosques.
He funded libraries.
He elevated Timbuktu into one of the greatest centers of learning in the medieval world.

This episode of Untold Africa challenges the narrative that Africa’s greatness is recent or borrowed. Long before modern empires, the Mali Empire stood as a global economic and intellectual force.

This is Untold Africa, where we explore the leaders, ideas, and moments that shaped Africa’s rise.

title: Africa Is the Future, But Only If We Change Our Mindset: MJ Jalloh || Masterminds Podcast EP40
description:

title: Atheist vs Christian vs Spiritual Thinker: Is Not Believing In God Causing More Harm Than Good?!
description:
Greg Koukl (Christian), Alex O’Connor (atheist), and Dr K (psychiatrist) explore why so many feel lost, whether God is the answer, how death anxiety drives our search for meaning, and how YOU can find direction…today.
